sector: Utilities / Independent Power Producers (merchant nuclear + thermal IPP, PJM)
bull_thesis: Talen is a scarce, grid-connected nuclear-IPP positioned at the single hardest physical bottleneck of the AI build-out: firm, carbon-free, already-permitted baseload inside a supply-starved PJM. The crown jewel — the ~2.2 GW Susquehanna nuclear station — is contracted to AWS for up to 1,920 MW front-of-the-meter through 2042 (~$1.4B annual revenue at full ramp by 2032) with an investment-grade hyperscaler counterparty, converting a historically volatile merchant into a long-dated annuity with SMR + uprate optionality on top. The numbers already inflected: Q1-2026 adjusted EBITDA $473M (>2x YoY), adjusted FCF $350M (~4x YoY), and a swing to GAAP profit. FY2026 guidance of $1.75-2.05B EBITDA / $0.98-1.18B FCF (ex-Cornerstone) implies ~83% EBITDA growth at midpoint, driven by the full year of the accretive Freedom/Guernsey ~3 GW thermal acquisitions, the June-2025 capacity/ROR price reset, hedges (~85% 2026), and the Amazon ramp. Management deleveraged aggressively (8.625% notes refinanced to ~6.25%, leverage 3.1x) and is buying back stock against a real FCF stream ($100M Q1, $1.9B left through 2028). The optionality is genuinely live: several '1+ GW' bilateral PPA opportunities in the pipeline, an Amazon SMR/uprate program adding net-new MW, and a Dec-2025 FERC order that re-opened a clearer co-location pathway. PJM weather-adjusted demand is growing ~3% on data-center load while capacity prices clear below newbuild economics — a structural scarcity that accrues to existing dispatchable owners. TLN is the merchant-nuclear pure-play with the cleanest hyperscaler contract and the most balance-sheet runway to keep consolidating.
bear_thesis: Almost everything already worked, and the price proves it: at ~$435 (mkt cap ~$19.8B, EV ~$24B+ pro-forma Cornerstone) TLN trades ~22x trailing and ~12-13x forward EV/EBITDA — a growth multiple on an asset base that is part single-asset nuclear and part commodity merchant thermal. The realized, contracted base alone (Amazon + capacity + hedges) does not support that multiple; the gap is an AI-scale-out call option that requires (a) the unsigned '1+ GW pipeline' to convert into real PPAs, and (b) the Amazon ramp to hit its long-dated 2032 full economics with no nuclear outage in between. Both are show-me. The original behind-the-meter Amazon premium was impaired by FERC's Nov-2024 rejection — the deal had to be restructured FTM — and the Dec-2025 FERC order re-litigating PJM co-location rules keeps regulatory tail risk live. Cornerstone (~2,451 MW thermal, $4B debt raised ahead of approvals) pushes pro-forma leverage toward the 3.5x ceiling while adding lower-multiple, carbon-exposed merchant MW that dilutes the pure-nuclear-scarcity framing — and management itself concedes capacity clears 'are not sufficient to stimulate new build,' i.e. the growth case rests entirely on bilateral hyperscaler demand staying durable. The whole thesis hinges on the SAME single macro factor — AI-capex durability — as the rest of the scale-out basket; an AI-capex guide-down would de-rate the entire nuclear-IPP cohort (CEG/VST/TLN) together. Single-asset Susquehanna concentration means one unplanned outage is a material FCF and headline shock. The double-digit-FCF-yield buyback narrative is stale: at $435 the forward FCF yield is mid-single-digit and the stock has outrun the repurchase math. This is a great asset at a price that already pays for the option.
sop_bull: ~$520-560/share. Parts: (1) Susquehanna nuclear contracted annuity — value the AWS 1,920 MW PPA (~$1.4B rev at full ramp, ~$700-800M run-rate EBITDA by 2032) + uncontracted nuclear at a CEG-like ~14-15x forward EBITDA given IG-counterparty contract duration ≈ ~$13-14B. (2) Legacy + Freedom/Guernsey + Cornerstone thermal/merchant fleet at ~8-9x EBITDA (merchant thermal multiple) on ~$0.9-1.0B of fleet EBITDA ≈ ~$8B. (3) Growth optionality (SMR + uprates + 1 incremental signed hyperscaler GW) ≈ ~$2-3B. Sum EV ~$23-25B less ~$5.5B pro-forma net debt = ~$18-20B equity → on ~45M sh ≈ ~$520-560. Overlay: $1.9B buyback at a mid-single FCF yield is mildly accretive.
sop_bear: ~$300-340/share. Parts: (1) Nuclear/Amazon contracted block at a more sober ~11-12x on ~$1.0B nuclear EBITDA ≈ ~$11B (no credit for unsigned pipeline). (2) Thermal merchant fleet at ~7x on ~$0.9B ≈ ~$6.3B. (3) Growth optionality discounted to ~$1B given FERC/co-location and capacity-market uncertainty. Sum EV ~$18-19B less ~$5.5B net debt (Cornerstone-loaded) = ~$13-14B equity → ~$300-340. This is the 'great asset, no option credit, leverage-at-ceiling' case.
sop_breakdown: Base case ~$415 mid. Nuclear/Amazon contracted annuity valued at ~13x forward EBITDA (premium to merchant for IG-contract duration, discount to CEG's ~14x for single-asset concentration) ≈ ~$13B EV; merchant thermal fleet (Freedom/Guernsey/Cornerstone + legacy) at ~8x ≈ ~$7.5B; growth optionality (SMR/uprate + a fraction of the unsigned 1+GW pipeline, probability-weighted) ≈ ~$1.5-2B. Sum EV ~$22-23B less ~$5.5B pro-forma net debt ≈ ~$17-18B equity ≈ ~$400-430/sh on ~45M shares. The current ~$435 sits at the TOP of this range — i.e. fair-to-slightly-rich, with the upside requiring NEW signed PPAs the market is already partially crediting.
risk_reward: Roughly symmetric-to-unfavorable from $435. Downside-to-bear-break ~$300-340 (−22% to −31%) if the pipeline stays unsigned / AI-capex wobbles / leverage bites; upside-to-bull ~$520-560 (+20% to +29%) only if incremental hyperscaler GW gets SIGNED and the Amazon ramp de-risks. Base fair value ~$415 is BELOW the current price. Net: ~1:1 at best, skewed negative because the market already credits the option that has to be earned.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED. The load-bearing catalyst — the AWS Susquehanna PPA — is FIRED/already-ran (announced/expanded Jun-2025, restructured FTM, the re-rate from $20s/$100s to $435 is largely spent; the ramp to full $1.4B economics is a long-dated 2032 grind, not a dated re-rating event). The forward catalyst — 'several 1+ GW' new hyperscaler PPAs — is SOFT_EXTENDED: real but UNSIGNED, serially-referenced as 'pipeline/opportunities,' single-binary on hyperscaler demand, with no committed date. The Cornerstone close (early H2-2026) is PENDING_HARD but it is an arb on an already-financed deal, not a re-rating driver. FERC co-location rulemaking (Dec-2025 order; PJM compliance filings Feb-2026) is an open regulatory process, double-edged. Net: no hard-dated, asymmetric forcing function inside the favorable window.
peer_comps_note: True scale-out peers are the merchant nuclear/IPP cohort levered to hyperscaler power: Constellation Energy (CEG, ~$80-90B cap, ~13x forward EV/EBITDA — the largest US nuclear fleet, Microsoft/Three Mile Island restart, the cohort's premium AI-power name), Vistra (VST, ~10x forward EV/EBITDA — nuclear + gas + retail, ERCOT/PJM, cheaper and more thermal-blended), and NRG Energy (gas/retail, lower-multiple, less nuclear-pure). TLN at ~12-13x forward EV/EBITDA ranks BETWEEN CEG and VST — richer than VST, near/just below CEG. On AI-capex exposure: TLN is among the PUREST (nuclear baseload + a marquee Amazon FTM contract) but is the SMALLEST and MOST CONCENTRATED (single crown-jewel asset vs CEG's diversified ~22-unit nuclear fleet). On GROWTH it screens fastest (acquisition + ramp-driven ~83% EBITDA growth guide) but that is inorganic/one-time. On RETURNS/MARGINS the merchant model is inherently more volatile and less proven post-emergence than CEG's regulated-ish contracted base. Verdict: TLN is priced like CEG-quality scarcity but carries VST-like merchant/concentration risk — the multiple leaves little margin of safety.
role_in_scaleout: TLN is a primary supplier of the single scarcest physical input in the entire AI build-out — firm, carbon-free, already-permitted nuclear baseload inside supply-starved PJM — anchored by a 1,920 MW front-of-the-meter Amazon PPA through 2042; that demand is highly durable (24/7 dispatchable power is non-substitutable for AI training/inference loads), but the equity's incremental value now depends on unsigned future PPAs and is already richly priced.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way here — it is looking the RIGHT way and has fully arrived. Consensus already prices TLN as a premier AI-power scarcity asset (~22x trailing, ~12-13x forward EV/EBITDA, between VST's ~10x and CEG's ~13x), already credits the Amazon FTM contract, and already extrapolates the '1+ GW pipeline' into the multiple. The expectations-arbitrage is therefore NEGATIVE, not positive: the consensus assumption most at risk is that incremental hyperscaler PPAs will keep getting SIGNED on Talen's terms and that the Amazon ramp hits 2032 full economics cleanly — both unproven, both single-binary on AI-capex durability. There is no hidden cheapness; the realized contracted base (Amazon + capacity + hedges) alone does not support $435, so the market is paying today for an option that still has to be earned. The mispricing, if any, is that the cohort (CEG/VST/TLN) shares ONE macro factor and would de-rate together on any AI-capex scare — a correlation the index-inclusion bid masks.
valley_of_death: Over the next 3-9 months the live risks are: (1) the Cornerstone $4B debt was raised AHEAD of regulatory approval — any approval slip leaves negative carry and pro-forma leverage pinned at the ~3.5x ceiling. (2) Susquehanna's spring-2026 refueling/reconfiguration outage (to convert the Amazon arrangement to FTM) is an idiosyncratic single-asset FCF/headline risk; any unplanned extension hits guidance directly. (3) No new signed PPA in 2026 would turn the '1+ GW pipeline' into a show-me liability and compress the option premium. (4) The dominant shared factor: any hyperscaler capex guide-down or AI-monetization scare (AI-capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth per the regime doc) would de-gross the entire nuclear-IPP cohort mechanically. (5) FERC/PJM co-location rulemaking outcomes could re-rate the co-location premium either way. FCF is strong and growing, so this is a multiple/sentiment valley, not a solvency one.
positioning_washout: Limited classic forced-selling setup — TLN pays no dividend, so there is no yield-base to flush on a cut. The base is growth/momentum + newly-added index/passive holders (Russell 1000/3000, S&P 400 inclusion in 2025 added a mechanical bid). The washout risk is the MIRROR of that: a momentum/AI-capex de-rating would see the same fast-money and thematic-ETF flows that drove TLN from the $100s to $435 reverse mechanically, with the index bid no longer additive once inclusion is lapped. There is no special-situation forced-seller dynamic to exploit on the long side; the cleaner setup would be to WAIT for an AI-capex-scare flush that resets the multiple back toward the ~$340-380 SoP support before the contracted growth re-asserts.
forcing_function: No hard-dated, asymmetric upside forcing function inside the favorable window. The nearest dated events are: Cornerstone close (early H2-2026, an arb not a re-rate), Susquehanna spring-2026 refuel/FTM reconfiguration (a risk event, not a catalyst), and the PJM co-location compliance/FERC process (open-ended). The genuine re-rating trigger would be a NEW signed 1+ GW hyperscaler PPA — but that is undated and SOFT. Absent a new signed contract, the next quarterly prints will be judged on whether the Amazon ramp and acquisition synergies hit the ~83% EBITDA growth guide; a miss is the more likely consensus-shattering event than an upside surprise.
moderator_conclusion: TLN is a genuinely high-quality asset on the right side of every relevant secular tail — carbon-free nuclear baseload is the scarcest physical input the AI scale-out must buy, and the Amazon FTM contract through 2042 converts a volatile merchant into a long-dated, IG-counterparty annuity with real SMR/uprate optionality. The forensic quality is strong (STRENGTHENING trajectory, credibility 4/5, deleveraged balance sheet, contracted cash flows that already inflected Q1-2026 EBITDA >2x). But the CRO verdict is PASS (C, conviction 2). Applying the regime: this is a name whose load-bearing catalyst has ALREADY FIRED and ALREADY RAN — the stock is up multiples off the Amazon news and now sits at ~$435, at the TOP of a ~$400-430 base-case SoP, ~22x trailing / ~12-13x forward EV/EBITDA (CEG-premium pricing with VST-like merchant/concentration risk). The regime doc explicitly penalizes already-fired/already-ran catalysts and prizes resolution-driver independence — and TLN fails that second test hardest: its entire forward case depends on the SAME single macro factor (AI-capex durability) as the whole scale-out basket, offering the Director near-zero diversification benefit and maximum cohort-correlation risk. The remaining upside requires UNSIGNED PPAs the market already partly credits; the downside (~$300-340) is an AI-capex de-rating that hits CEG/VST/TLN together. Risk/reward is ~1:1 skewed negative from here. Correct posture: WATCHLIST for an AI-capex-scare flush back toward the SoP-support ~$340-380, then own the contracted compounder — do NOT chase the option at full price today. value_conviction is 3 (not lower): stripped of catalyst/regime overlay, at the current price the name is fair-to-slightly-rich versus an honest SoP, with genuine forensic quality but no margin of safety — a hold-quality, not a buy-quality, valuation. The NARROW-but-WIDENING moat and manageable (arguably winner-side) secular threat keep value_conviction from being capped lower; the full price keeps it from being higher.
interrogator_dossier:
# Talen Energy (TLN) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator, Opus 4.8 · scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN) · nuclear/merchant IPP · web-sourced (Q1 2026 call + FY2025 release; no bundled transcripts). Price ~$435, mkt cap ~$19.8B (mid-Jun 2026).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Talen's story has compressed three distinct identities into ~24 months. (i) Post-bankruptcy merchant deleveraging — fresh out of Chapter 11 (2023), the early narrative was 'clean up the balance sheet, sell non-core, buy back stock at a double-digit FCF yield.' (ii) The nuclear-AI pivot — the March-2024 sale of the co-located ~960 MW Cumulus/Susquehanna data-center campus to AWS reframed Talen as THE grid-connected nuclear-IPP play on hyperscaler power. (iii) Scale-up consolidator — through FY2025/Q1-2026 the emphasis shifted to bolting on dispatchable thermal MW (Freedom + Guernsey ~3 GW closed Q4-2025; Cornerstone ~2,451 MW announced) to diversify away from single-asset nuclear and build a larger contracting book to sell to data centers.

The load-bearing thesis evolution is the June-2025 restructuring of the Amazon deal from behind-the-meter (BTM) to front-of-the-meter (FTM): 1,920 MW from Susquehanna through 2042, ramping to full volume by 2032, ~$1.4B annual revenue at full quantity. This was forced — FERC rejected the original co-located ISA expansion in Nov-2024 — yet management re-narrated a regulatory setback into a larger, longer, grid-connected contract with SMR + uprate optionality bolted on. The growth driver quietly introduced in late-2025/Q1-2026 is multiple '1+ GW' PPA opportunities in the pipeline (Terry Nutt) — prospective, not signed. The driver quietly de-emphasized: standalone merchant spark-spread upside, now reframed as 'hedged 85% 2026.'

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

- 'AWS PPA ramp is driving results': Q1-26 adj EBITDA $473M vs $200M Q1-25 (>2x); adj FCF $350M vs $87M (~4x); GAAP net income +$63M vs -$135M. CORROBORATED — but the bulk of the jump is Freedom/Guernsey acquisitions + higher capacity/ROR revenue (live Jun-2025) + spark spreads, not yet mostly Amazon (ramp only begins post spring-2026 refuel).
- FY2026 guide $1.75-2.05B EBITDA / $0.98-1.18B FCF vs FY2025 actual $1,035M EBITDA / $524M FCF implies ~83% EBITDA growth at midpoint (~$1.9B). PLAUSIBLE BUT ACQUISITION-LOADED — the step-up is full-year acquired MW + capacity-price reset + Amazon ramp, not organic. Excludes Cornerstone (upside).
- 'Double-digit FCF yield funds buybacks': $100M repurchased Q1-26; $1.9B left through 2028; ~$1.08B FCF midpoint vs ~$19.8B cap = ~5.5% yield. HALF-TRUE/DATED — 'double-digit' reflects a lower prior share price; at $435 forward FCF yield is mid-single-digit. The stock has run away from the buyback math.
- 'Disciplined leverage <3.5x': net leverage 3.1x at 3/31/26 (ex-Cornerstone); $4B new notes at ~6.25% blended, redeemed $1.2B 8.625% notes (+$40M/yr savings). CORROBORATED — genuine cost-of-debt improvement; but Cornerstone adds $4B gross debt toward the ceiling.
- 'Capacity clears insufficient to stimulate new build' (Cole): HONEST TELL — management candidly says merchant economics alone don't justify newbuild; the entire growth case depends on bilateral hyperscaler PPAs, not the capacity market.

Where the story is richer than the numbers: the '1+ GW pipeline' and SMR optionality carry a large slice of the equity narrative but contribute $0 signed revenue today. Where poorer: the realized EBITDA jump is real and largely contracted, so the base is more defensible than a typical merchant.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

Rising / offensive. Management moved from defensive deleveraging to confident capital deployment. Q1-26: hyperscaler 'capital is hitting the ground in tangible infrastructure' (Nutt); PJM demand '~3% incremental deliveries weather-adjusted'; 'significant interest in connecting to the grid... taking power as soon as possible.' Cornerstone financing framed as speed — raising $4B 'ahead of regulatory approvals... own the asset sooner and benefit more from the peak summer period' (Cole). The one defensive note is candor on capacity-market inadequacy ('the gap is not a couple of bucks; it is pretty large'), reading as credibility, not weakness.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

Medium-High. Reaffirmed FY2026 at Q1 (no cut/raise), closed Freedom/Guernsey on schedule, executed the Amazon FTM restructuring after the FERC setback, pre-financed Cornerstone. 'Say it, do it' on M&A and balance sheet since emergence. Risk: guidance is heavily acquisition- and ramp-dependent (a deal slip or Amazon ramp delay flows straight through), and full Amazon economics ($1.4B) only by 2032. No sandbagging; excluding Cornerstone from guidance is mild conservatism.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Recurring push: 'where are the NEW data-center contracts?' — management converts to pipeline language ('several 1+ GW opportunities') rather than signed deals, a deflection that becomes a show-me liability if no new PPA prints in 2026. Second: capacity-market design / RVP backstop — management concedes merchant economics largely don't support growth capex, leaning on bilaterals. Third: co-location regulatory risk — the Dec-2025 FERC order directing PJM to write new co-location/BTM rules is double-edged (re-opened a pathway but re-litigates the rules the Amazon deal routed around).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: Contracted IG-counterparty cash flows (Amazon to 2042); genuine deleveraging + cost-of-debt reduction (8.625%->~6.25%, leverage 3.1x); disciplined buyback + index inclusion (mechanical bid); SMR + uprate optionality = net-new MW into supply-starved PJM.
RED: Marginal equity dollar rests on UNSIGNED pipeline; valuation outran the buyback math (~22x trailing / ~12-13x forward EV/EBITDA growth multiple on partly-merchant base); single-asset Susquehanna concentration (outage = idiosyncratic FCF shock); Cornerstone pushes leverage to ceiling while adding carbon-exposed thermal; thesis shares ONE macro driver (AI-capex) with the whole basket (low resolution-driver independence).

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Vocabulary shifted from 'co-located / behind-the-meter' (2024) to 'front-of-the-meter / grid-connected' (2025-26) — the regulatory path of least resistance now runs through the grid; the original BTM premium thesis was impaired by FERC. 'Large load contracting opportunities' as the rationale for buying thermal (Cornerstone) signals Talen wants to sell hyperscalers a firm 24/7 PORTFOLIO, not just nuclear baseload — strategic widening that dilutes the 'pure nuclear scarcity' framing. Pre-funding Cornerstone debt 'ahead of regulatory approvals' is conviction-signaling but raises carry/execution risk.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Trajectory: STRENGTHENING (contracted cash flows growing, balance sheet de-risked, optionality real) — but the single most important thing to weigh is how much of the AI-power optionality is already in the price. At ~$435 the stock prices a successful multi-year ramp plus unsigned incremental PPAs; the realized contracted base alone does not support the multiple — the gap is the AI-scale-out call option, and TLN has already run hard on it.

MOAT — NARROW, WIDENING. A regulatory-license + scarce-asset moat: Susquehanna is one of a finite set of large, already-permitted, baseload-firm, carbon-free nuclear units inside PJM with available interconnection — genuinely scarce, being contracted into a long-dated IG-counterparty annuity (widening the durable base). NARROW not WIDE because the thermal acquisitions are commodity merchant MW with no moat, the economics still depend on capacity/energy prices and a single hyperscaler counterparty, and returns are not yet a demonstrated high-and-rising ROIC (post-emergence, acquisition-distorted). Moat_trend WIDENING via contract duration + SMR/uprate adds.

SECULAR THREAT — none-to-manageable, arguably a secular WINNER. Talen sits on the right side of the energy-transition and AI-power tails: carbon-free nuclear baseload is the scarcest input the AI build-out must buy. The structural risk is not terminal-value erosion but commoditization of the optionality (everyone now knows nuclear MW are valuable -> scarcity rent competed/regulated away) plus single-factor exposure to AI-capex durability. The thermal sleeve carries mild energy-transition-loser exposure but is a minority of the base and is being contracted/dispatched into a tight grid.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: WIDENING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found. The adversarial attack vectors all FAIL to break the (bear/PASS) thesis: (a) STALE-ANCHOR — NO; sop_fair_value is built on CURRENT data: Q1-2026 actuals (filed in the 2026-05-05 8-K), reaffirmed FY2026 guidance, the post-restructuring Amazon FTM contract (Jun-2025), and the announced Cornerstone deal — not pre-event/pre-guidance financials. (b) NUMBER-TRUTH — every load-bearing figure verifies against primary SEC 8-Ks and company IR: Q1-26 adj EBITDA $473M, adj FCF $350M, GAAP NI +$63M; FY2026 guide $1,750-2,050M EBITDA / $980-1,180M FCF (ex-Cornerstone); net leverage 3.1x ex-Cornerstone with a 3.5x YE2026 target; Cornerstone 2,451 MW ($3.45B price, $4B raised); Amazon 1,920 MW FTM through 2042 (~$18B lifetime rev, ramp 840-1,200 MW by 2029 / 1,680-1,920 MW by 2032); ~45.4M shares; price ~$435. (c) THESIS-WEAKNESS — CONFIRMED in the bear's favor: the '1+ GW pipeline' is INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED as still UNSIGNED (no new hyperscaler PPA beyond Amazon found in any primary/news source as of Jun-2026), so the marginal equity dollar does rest on an unearned option; single-customer (Amazon) + single-asset (Susquehanna) + single-macro-factor (AI-capex) concentration all hold. (d) HIDDEN-DISQUALIFIER — the Cornerstone $4B debt raised AHEAD of regulatory approvals (FERC/IURC pending; HSR cleared Mar-2026) pinning pro-forma leverage at the ~3.5x ceiling is real and verified. The bear case (C / PASS / watchlist-for-a-flush) SURVIVES the kill-check intact.
skeptic_corrections: One non-load-bearing correction: the bull_thesis/dossier state the new notes refinanced to '~6.25%'. The actual Cornerstone financing (priced 2026-04-17) is $1.5B of 6.125% senior notes due 2031 + $2.5B of 6.375% senior notes due 2033 (blended ~6.3%), redeeming $1.2B of 8.625% notes. Cosmetic; does not change leverage or the verdict. Also note the Cornerstone purchase price is $3.45B (the $4B figure is gross debt RAISED, which also funds the 8.625% redemption) — the dossier conflates the two loosely but the bear's leverage-to-ceiling point is correct.
skeptic_evidence: SEC 8-K, Talen Energy Q1 2026 earnings release (filed 2026-05-05): adj EBITDA $473M, adj FCF $350M, GAAP NI $63M; FY2026 reaffirmed $1,750-2,050M EBITDA / $980-1,180M FCF excl. Cornerstone; net leverage 3.1x (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001622536/000162253626000035)

SEC 8-K, Talen senior notes pricing (2026-04-17): $1.5B 6.125% notes due 2031 + $2.5B 6.375% notes due 2033 (~$4B) to fund the $3.45B Cornerstone 2,451 MW acquisition (Lawrenceburg 1,120 / Waterford 875 / Darby 456) and redeem $1.2B 8.625% notes; close early H2-2026, FERC/IURC pending, HSR cleared Mar-2026 (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622536/000162253626000030)

SEC 8-K / Talen IR (2025-06-11): Amazon AWS 1,920 MW front-of-the-meter PPA from Susquehanna through 2042, ~$18B lifetime revenue, ramp 840-1,200 MW by 2029 and 1,680-1,920 MW by 2032; restructured from behind-the-meter after the Nov-2024 FERC rejection (ir.talenenergy.com / sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001622536/000162828025030559)

Web/news verification (Jun-2026): no NEW signed hyperscaler/data-center PPA beyond the Amazon deal located in any primary or news source — the 'several 1+ GW' pipeline remains UNSIGNED; ~45.4M shares outstanding, price ~$435 — corroborating the bear's 'option already priced, not yet earned' core claim (powermag.com; nasdaq.com TLN)

scale.role: Scarce grid-connected nuclear-IPP (2.2 GW Susquehanna) in supply-starved PJM; AWS PPA anchor.
scale.rationale: Real scarce-asset franchise (2.2 GW Susquehanna, AWS 1,920 MW FTM through 2042, Cornerstone 2,451 MW pending) — a strong LEVER, duplicative of VST/CEG within nuclear-IPP and with thinner contracting/more leverage. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, the only correction is a cosmetic note-rate detail (the '1+ GW pipeline' is verified still UNSIGNED, reinforcing the bear), so a ~8pt haircut off a ~64 franchise. The AWS re-rate is spent; base ~$415 ~5% below spot; wait.
scale.demand_durability: High — nuclear baseload in PJM, but more leverage and a thinner contracted book than CEG/VST.
